_cap_override.note:
PROMOTED from Preferred to Mandatory per 2026-05-15 Sales/CRM walkthrough
with evidence pipeline grounding. Also rewrote name + description +
vendor_question per docs/solcat_writing_guide.md (third-person singular
present, no '&'/'/'/contractions, outcome-focused, ≤15-word Type-2 sub-bullet).
Solution evidence (3 vendors, verbatim from feature pages):
  - Odoo Sales: "Pricelists — Compute the correct price based on customer
    conditions, such as region and price tier"; "Discounts — Apply
    discounts on any quotation line item"; "Coupons and Promotions"
  - MS D365 Business Central: "Set up how you do sales, such as prices
    and discounts, customer price and discount groups"
  - ERPNext Selling: pricing rules + customer-group discounts
IMDA evidence: 4 ERP suite sol cats verbatim mandate "Set up and edit
prices and discounts for different customer groups" in mandatory Qs.
SG SME need: universal — any B2B/retail SME needs customer-tier pricing
and promo discounts.
Evidence files: data/vendor_pricing/_features/odoo_online__sales-features.md,
ms_dynamics_365_business_central__sales-manage-sales.md, erpnext__selling*.md.

Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your customer information is accurate, accessible, and secure across all your devices and team members. Singapore SMEs rely on this to comply with PDPA regulations, enable remote and mobile sales teams, and integrate with accounting software like Xero or QuickBooks. A typical scenario: your sales rep on the road can access the complete customer record on their phone, make a call directly from the CRM, and log the interaction—all while staying compliant with data consent rules.

Sales Transactions.narrative: This family handles the core mechanics of selling—quotes, orders, invoices, pricing, and commissions—so you can process deals faster and pay your team fairly. For Singapore SMEs, it eliminates manual spreadsheet juggling and ensures consistent pricing across customers. Example: a distributor can apply volume discounts automatically, generate a professional quote in minutes, and track commission payouts monthly without error.

CRM & Relationship.narrative: This family tracks every prospect and customer interaction so your team never loses a lead or forgets a follow-up, and new team members can pick up where others left off. Singapore SMEs benefit from structured lead qualification and a shared view of the sales pipeline, reducing deal slippage and improving forecasting. Scenario: a prospect calls back after three months; your team instantly sees all prior conversations and can pick up the conversation without missing context.

Marketing.narrative: This family automates customer communication across email, social media, and your website, so you can nurture leads and retain customers without hiring a full marketing team. Singapore SMEs can build brand awareness and drive repeat purchases through targeted campaigns and customer feedback loops. Example: a beauty retailer sends a birthday discount email to loyal customers, captures feedback via NPS surveys, and uses that data to improve product recommendations.

Commerce.narrative: This family enables you to sell online—whether direct-to-consumer, B2B, or through marketplaces like Shopee and Lazada—while keeping inventory and customer data in sync. Singapore SMEs can reach customers 24/7 and reduce manual order entry by connecting multiple sales channels to one system. Scenario: a fashion brand sells on their own website, Shopee, and Lazada; inventory updates in real-time across all three, and a customer can earn loyalty points regardless of where they shop.

Customer Service.narrative: This family organises customer support requests into a ticketing system so nothing falls through the cracks and customers get faster, consistent help. Singapore SMEs can track SLAs, build a knowledge base to reduce repeat questions, and measure team performance. Example: a software vendor receives support requests via email, chat, and phone; all are logged as tickets, assigned to the right person, and tracked until resolution.

Analytics.narrative: This family turns raw CRM data into dashboards and insights so you can see what's working, predict which deals will close, and spot at-risk customers before they leave. Singapore SMEs can make faster, data-driven decisions on pricing, hiring, and marketing spend. Scenario: a sales manager sees that deals in the 'proposal' stage have a 40% close rate and 60-day cycle; they use that to forecast Q4 revenue and adjust team targets accordingly.
